content.problemStatement:
You are given a simple graph with NNN nodes and MMM edges. The graph may not be connected.

Each edge in the graph has an integer weight assigned to it and each node is **coloured** either white or black.

In one operation, you can choose an edge that has a white node at one end and a black node at the other, and reduce its weight by one. After this operation, the colour of both the nodes at the ends of the chosen edge are swapped, i.e. the white node becomes black and the black node becomes white. The edge weight can become zero or negative after the operation.

Your task is to find the **minimum** number of operations such that:


the edge weight of all the edges becomes **equal**, and;
each node gets to the same colour as it started.
If it is not possible, print −1-1−1.

**NOTE:** A simple graph is a graph that does not have more than one edge between any two nodes and no edge starts and ends at the same node.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of nodes and edges of the graph, respectively.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, ithi^{th}ith of which is 000 if ithi^{th}ith node is white, 111 if ithi^{th}ith node is black.
The next MMM lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these MMM lines contains three space-separated integers uiu_iui​, viv_ivi​ and wiw_iwi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ with edge weight wiw_iwi​.




Output Format
For each test case, print the minimum number of operations on a new line, or −1-1−1 if not possible.
constraints: Each node is either white or black.
The graph formed is a simple graph.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1:

Initial graph
After applying the following operations: (3,1),(3, 1),(3,1), (1,2),(1, 2),(1,2), (5,4),(5, 4),(5,4), (2,3),(2, 3),(2,3), and (4,5)(4, 5)(4,5)

Final graph
It can be proved that this is the minimum number of operations.
Test case 2: In this test case all the nodes are black and hence no operation can be applied to reach the desired condition.
Test case 3: In this test case it can be proved that the desired condition can not be reached by any number of operations.
